title: sprint crashes when delegate produces no parsed_output
source_context: Triggered during sprint of 6-ticket batch on Eren Rent project. Adventure #1 succeeded but failed quality gate (left in_review). Adventure #2 timed out at 600s, then crashed the orchestrator instead of marking ticket blocked/timeout and continuing.
description: orchestrate.py:939 update_sprint_state calls parsed_output.get('description', '')[:300] without checking if parsed_output itself is None. When a delegated Morty times out (e.g. 600s frontend-morty timeout on EREN-023), the parsed result is None, triggering TypeError: 'NoneType' object is not subscriptable and crashing the entire sprint loop mid-iteration.
proposed_changes: Guard the .get() call: if parsed_output is None: parsed_output = {}. Also propagate timeout cleanly so the ticket gets marked 'blocked' with reason='timeout' rather than crashing the whole sprint. Consider also: on timeout, leave the session_id in the ticket for --resume.
